title: Financial Business Analyst BI & Controlling (m/w/d)
required_skills: Degree in business administration, business informatics, industrial engineering or comparable
Professional experience in BI, controlling, or FP&A environment
High expertise in Power BI (semantic models and reporting)
Experience with AI systems for automation and openness to new technologies
Deep understanding of P&L, cost structures, and working capital; analytical and structured working style
Ability to communicate complex issues to target groups; enjoy working in interdisciplinary teams
bonus_skills: Experience related to supply chain KPIs and Integrated Business Planning (IBP)
matched_skills: Power BI (PL-300 certified) — directly and strongly satisfies the Power BI expertise requirement; semantic models and DAX reporting implied by certification
Master of Management & Entrepreneurship (Leuphana) — satisfies degree requirement
BI/reporting experience: built multi-dimensional budget variance dashboards at HSBC; KPI reporting at Hapag-Lloyd — partially satisfies BI/controlling experience requirement
AI tool usage (ChatGPT, Claude) in CV — partially satisfies AI systems/automation openness
Analytical and structured working style: KPI framework design, data storytelling at HSBC — satisfies analytical style requirement
Communication of complex data to senior stakeholders: C-suite escalation, sprint review reports — satisfies communication requirement
Fluent English — satisfies B1 English minimum
missing_skills: German B1 falls short of the required C1 minimum — this is an explicit hard requirement
No SQL skills — explicitly required for data querying and analysis
No formal controlling or FP&A role experience (the HSBC role was PMO budget tracking, not financial accounting/controlling)
No P&L ownership, cost structure analysis, or working capital management experience
No experience with supply chain KPIs or IBP (bonus gap)
core_responsibilities: Build and develop Power BI reports and data/semantic models including KPI definition as unified management basis
Analyse operational and financial KPIs (costs, margins, volumes, inventories) including variance, root-cause and structural analyses
Financial valuation of analysis results (P&L, cost and working capital effects) and derivation of action recommendations
Further develop BI-supported planning and control processes in FP&A environment and standardise reporting/controlling structures
recommended_emphasis: Lead strongly with Power BI PL-300 certification — this is the single highest-weight requirement and the candidate has the certification
Reframe the HSBC budget tracking role: emphasise multi-dimensional variance analysis, burn-rate modelling, and dashboard design as FP&A-adjacent work
Address the German language gap in the cover letter — B1 is below C1; the candidate should indicate active improvement (e.g., enrolled in language course)
Note the SQL gap honestly — if the candidate has any SQL exposure, even basic querying, mention it; otherwise flag as a learning priority
summary: Moderate match at 50.0%. The Power BI PL-300 certification is a strong differentiator and directly addresses the primary technical requirement. The HSBC budget governance and dashboard work provides adjacent FP&A evidence. However, the German C1 requirement is an explicit hard barrier (candidate is B1), and the absence of SQL skills is a meaningful gap for a BI/controlling role. Worth applying with a strong cover letter addressing the German language development — the PL-300 may compensate if the hiring manager has flexibility on language level.
